audit_status.source_base: Greek Hebrews text tradition, with extensive Septuagint citation network; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Son as final speech, radiance/imprint language, purification, enthronement, and catena of Scripture citations.

verses.restored: In many parts and many ways long ago the Divine spoke to the ancestors in the prophets,
but in these last days spoke to us in Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the ages.
He, being radiance of glory and exact imprint of his reality, bearing all things by the word of his power, having made purification of sins/misalignments, sat down at right hand of Majesty on high,
having become as much greater than the messengers as the name he has inherited is more excellent than theirs.
For to which of the messengers did he ever say: You are my Son; today I have begotten you? And again: I will be to him Father, and he will be to me Son?
And again, when he brings the firstborn into the inhabited world, he says: Let all messengers of Divine worship him.
And concerning the messengers he says: The One making his messengers winds, and his servants flame of fire.
But concerning the Son: Your throne, O Divine, is into the age of the age, and the scepter of straightness is scepter of your reign.
You loved righteousness/justice and hated lawlessness; because of this the Divine, your Divine, anointed you with oil of gladness beyond your companions.
And: You, Lord, according to beginnings, founded the earth, and heavens are works of your hands.
They will perish, but you remain; and all will become old like garment,
and like robe you will roll them up, like garment they will be changed; but you are the same, and your years will not fail.
And to which of the messengers has he ever said: Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies footstool of your feet?
Are they not all serving spirits, sent out into service because of those about to inherit salvation/healing?

translation_notes: Hebrews 1 is a Scripture-catena built largely from Greek Jewish Scriptures; citation texture should remain visible.
Son-language is enthronement/inheritance/revelation language, not anti-angel polemic detached from Jewish apocalyptic context.
Purification of sins is mentioned before priesthood is fully developed.
safety_note: Do not frame Hebrews as God replacing Judaism with Christianity. The chapter argues through Israel's own Scriptures about the Son's exalted identity.
